title: Email Forwarding Rule and Auto-Reply Audit
dataSources: Exchange/M365 mailbox rule and forwarding audit logs
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="o365:audit"
| search (Operation="New-InboxRule" OR Operation="Set-Mailbox" OR Message="ForwardingRule")
| table _time, UserId, Operation, Parameters, ClientIP
| sort -_time
description: Unauthorized forwarding rules can exfiltrate mail; auto-replies may leak sensitive info. Auditing rule changes supports security and compliance.
value: Unauthorized forwarding rules can exfiltrate mail; auto-replies may leak sensitive info. Auditing rule changes supports security and compliance.
implementation: Ingest mailbox rule and forwarding change events from M365/Exchange. Alert on new forwarding rules or external redirects. Report on rule changes by user and time.
visualization: Table (rule changes), Timeline (events), Bar chart (changes by user).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`, `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`, Exchange audit.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: Exchange/M365 mailbox rule and forwarding audit logs.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Ingest mailbox rule and forwarding change events from M365/Exchange. Alert on new forwarding rules or external redirects. Report on rule changes by user and time.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="o365:audit"
| search (Operation="New-InboxRule" OR Operation="Set-Mailbox" OR Message="ForwardingRule")
| table _time, UserId, Operation, Parameters, ClientIP
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Email Forwarding Rule and Auto-Reply Audit** ŌĆö Unauthorized forwarding rules can exfiltrate mail; auto-replies may leak sensitive info. Auditing rule changes supports security and compliance.

Documented **Data sources**: Exchange/M365 mailbox rule and forwarding audit logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`, `Splunk_TA_MS_O365`, Exchange audit. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: o365:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype="o365:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
ŌĆó Pipeline stage (see **Email Forwarding Rule and Auto-Reply Audit**): table _time, UserId, Operation, Parameters, ClientIP
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Compare with CUCM RTMT, Cisco collaboration admin, or the vendorŌĆÖs own status and CDR/CMR views for the same cluster and time window.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (rule changes), Timeline (events), Bar chart (changes by user).
grandmaExplanation: We use this to stay ahead of email and collaboration problems, so the team is not the last to know when something drifts or breaks.
knownFalsePositives: Legitimate auto-reply and forward rules for coverage, PTO, and shared functional mailboxes.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
